entry.title: Re: bapi_po_change issue in looping when mass deleting th...
entry.content:
Did you analyze the RETURN table from BAPI_PO_CHANGE to identify any specific errors or warnings related to the deletion process.

Make sure that you are providing the correct key fields in the PO_ITEM and PO_ITEM_EXT structures. For deleting an item, you need to specify the item number and the appropriate fields.

entry.title: Re: DP Agent config tool - Exception
entry.content: Usually there won't be such error, not sure what's the dp agent version? Which user did you use to start agentcli? Please make sure the user has the full access, also try latest version of DP agent installation

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Embedded Analytics: Custom Analytical Queries
entry.content:
Overview:

In this blog, we will explore about Analytical Queries and how standard embedded analytics coverage with option to additionally enhance with Custom Analytical Queries. This is a central object of most of the Analytical use cases in S/4HANA. Custom Analytical queries transforms and organizes raw data delivered from business document into a meaningful report. SAP S/4HANA comes with long list of pre-defined queries that are released for customer usage. However, there are always a customer specific queries to provide data to answer special questions and address specific use cases. To address this need Custom Analytical Queries are created. This extensibility feature is available in both Public / Private editions.

As shown in the above diagram, Analytical Query Provides data to:

  • KPIs.
  • Multidimensional reports
  • Sap Analytics Cloud Stories/Dashboards

Analytics specialist role is required to create these queries. Creating Analytical queries and maintaining the existing queries are the main tasks of the analytics specialist. We can create Custom Analytics Queries using Custom Analytics App in the Query design business catalogue.

Custom Analytics Queries are created based on below options:

Custom Analytical Queries

Step 1: Access the Custom Analytical Queries App in S/4HANA cloud.

 

Step 2: Create Custom Analytical Queries by selecting existing Data source.

As depicted in the above diagram, Once launched the custom analytical query app, we will be presented with the above screen. In the top right corner select the create button and provide the Custom query name and underlying data source. In this example we are trying to use the existing Released cube “C_ANALYZESUPRUTILZNCUBE” and build custom query on top of it. Please note the system will automatically prefix YY1 in the query names.

Step 3: Providing Necessary Information in “General” tab.

Step 4: Selecting Required fields in the custom query.

We will have to pick the required fields for the custom query from the source data source. In this case we have selected all the fields from the source. Please note the numeric values are automatically grouped under “measure structure”. The other fields are in the “Free” Section.

Step 5: Selecting Display fields for the Custom Query.

In the “Display” tab pick the required fields as shown above. Please note, for dimensions pick the Row, so that we can analyse based on that specific field. In this example we have selected “Country” as a dimension to analyse the number of contracts from country wise. You can leave rest of the dimensions in the “Free” characteristic section itself. so that – the end user can pick and choose dynamic fields based on the changing requirement.

Step 6: Selecting “Filters” for the Query

In the Last tab – “Filter” Section include the necessary filters for the result set to appear in the report. As shown above, select the appropriate radio buttons and apply the filtering criteria.  The user also can select User input values as a filtering option. In this example we have restricted the result by excluding a particular country code.

Once all the relevant fields are filtered, click save draft and publish as shown above.After Publishing, Browse the below Custom Analytical Queries list and provide the required query name and double click to access the Query and its results.

 

Execute the Query and the Country wise Result shown as below:

 

 

In the above screen, you will find ‘Data Analysis’, if the end user wish to see the result in the form of tabular format. ‘Graphical display’ is the one which shown above. We also have Query Technical information about this Custom Query. The details of query and underlying data source are displayed in this tab.

In general, these queries are consumed by the reporting tools (i.e. SAC Dashboard) so look and feel of the output and report will change based on the Reporting Tool.

Conclusion:

In this blog, we have discussed about accessing Analytical Queries and creating the custom analytical queries. Hope this demonstrates ‘Custom Analytical Queries’ creation process in a step-by-step manner. This SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics feature will greatly benefit our customers to implement the dynamic requirements of their business.

Other Useful Blog Links for the Related Topics are:

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/s4hana-embedded-analytics-analytical-list-page-report-step-by-step/ba-p/13738327
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/s-4hana-embedded-analytics-extensibility-process-amp-custom-cds-view/ba-p/13793312
  • Refer for More details from SAP Portal Help Documentation : Custom Analytical Queries

Thank You for your time! Happy Learning!!!

entry.title: Re: Message Header in Content modifier SAP CPI
entry.content:
By default, you can search the message logs by message id, correlation id, application id, sender, receiver and message type. For application id you need to set the header SAP_ApplicationID in a content modifier, for sender would be SAP_Sender, for receiver SAP_Receiver, and for message type SAP_MessageType.

All other headers are not displayed in the logs unless you switch on trace, though you still can't use them for search. If you need further parameters for search you can define so called custom header properties. Check out the design guidelines to learn how this works: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/use-custom-header-properties-to-search-for-message-processing-logs

If you are a beginner to SAP Integration Suite as stated by you, then I would recommend the guidelines to you anyway: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/integration-flow-design-guidelines

Alex

Hi,

Key users can extend the Manage RFQs app in the Custom Fields app and the Custom Logic app using the following business contexts:

  • Procurement: Purchasing Document

  • Procurement: Purchasing Document Item

Please refer to the content in help document: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/5db606b1c5a0459ab07ffed4661e42b8.html?version=LATEST

Thank you,

Krithika

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Finance for Group Reporting - Currency Translation Rounding Rules & Errors:
entry.content:
Hi Hengye,

Yes, Scenario 1 in the Blog is relevant to SAP Note 3474581 as the Note deals with Some FS items not included in the rounding selection. The Other Note 3233850 deals with Local Currency(LC) Imbalance whereas my Scenario 2 deals with Rounding Error during Currency Translation from Local Currency to Group Currency when the user skips the RE Calculation before CT.

Regards,

Priyanka

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 PDFViewer Not Working : Underlying iframe was...
entry.content:
Hi @WouterLemaire,

In the end, I tested on another computer with CHROME and there was no problem.
So the problem seemed to come from my own CHROME.

However, in my CHROME my settings were OK.
I've reset the "chrome://settings/content/pdfDocuments" and after that the problem was solved.

For the point with the IFrame I tried and it was OK but I wanted to do it with the PDFViewer.
I wanted to stay with something standard.

Thanks for your feedback 😉
YouriC

entry.title: BYD2408 Add Customer name&ID to email subject for layout "Account Statement with Due Date List"
entry.content:
Hi.

I am are trying to add Customer name & Customer ID to the subject of form template "Account Statement with Due Date List".
Specifically it is to the the, United Kingdom - English , United States - English and France - French version of the form.
I donwloaded the form and its scheme that includes the XML of the data structure.

Here are examples of how i tried editing the Subject :
*** [FormTradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement-BusinessPartnerInternalID]
*** [TradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement-BusinessPartnerInternalID]
*** [FormTradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement-FormTradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement-BusinessPartnerInternalID]

I also tried with CompanyID:
*** [FormTradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement-CompanyID]
*** [TradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement-CompanyID]
*** [FormTradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement-FormTradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement-CompanyID]

With all those I did not see the actual values in the emails that were send.
This included making a new document and seeing the email output as it arrived in the mail or generating another output of an existing Balance Confirmation and seeing its output in the mail.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2408 Add Customer name&ID to email subject for lay...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

The placeholder input is incorrectly. The following is a correct example.

test 123 [TradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement-BusinessPartnerInternalID] [TradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement-CompanyID].

When input the placeholder, the path in "[]" should be started after the part "<MessageHeader> ....  </MessageHeader>" in the XML.

For example, the following is a part of the original XML. When input place holder, ignore the contents until </MessageHeader>. The start should be TradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement

------------------------------------------------

<n0:FormTradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatementNotification
    xmlns:n0="http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal20/Global"
    xmlns:prx="urn:sap.com:proxy:LDI:/1SAI/TAE0FBC2F1E449FB78B1491:804">
    <MessageHeader>
        <CreationDateTime/>
    </MessageHeader>
    <TradeReceivablesPayablesAccountStatement>
        <CompanyID>.......</CompanyID>

------------------------------------------------

 

Best Regards,

SAP Support,

Cariford

entry.title: Re: How to get the Full Statement_String from Expensive S...
entry.content:
Hi @kirankumar440 

What you see here is the log of the parameterised call to the text search service.

I don't think you will be able to see the parameters as they are usually hidden for security reasons, as they may contain sensitive information.

You might try increasing the log level in the traces to see if you can see some more detail.
Or maybe there is the execution plan in the cache, look in the M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE view for the STATEMENT_STRING field to see if there is something with TREXviaDBSL.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Issue with Duplicate Invoice Check in Vendor Invoice...
entry.content:
Hi Santosh,

Thank you for your response. While the duplicate check functions correctly for all invoices posted through FB60 and MIRO, my concern is specific to our client's setup with VIM (Vendor Invoice Management). For certain business verticals where invoices are processed through VIM, if a user attempts to repost the same invoice manually via FB60, the system bypasses the duplicate invoice check validation. This issue seems to occur exclusively with invoices initially processed through VIM.

entry.title: Maintenance Plan and Measuring Point cannot be maintained in PRT equipment master
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I am trying to set up the PRT usage-based Maintenance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. The purpose is to update measuring document automatically after production order confirmation.

 

During the creation of equipment master data, I cannot find the Maintenance Plan and Measuring Point to fill in data. I try to do some research how to visible those fields but nothing found.

Can you help me to check and guide how to visible those fields? Thank you.PRT equipment master - missing fields

entry.title: Re: SAC Calculate after aggregation based on datasphere
entry.content:
As you need a calculation AFTER aggregation, it must be done in SAC or in Analytic Model.

Create a Restricted Measure in the Analytics Model for CY and LY, and then create a Calculated Measure with the CY - LY, this will be calculated AFTER aggregation and tranlated as

AGG(X) WHERE CY  - AGG(X) WHERE PY

Regards,

entry.title: Accelerating Digital Transformation: An Insider's Journey to SAP Cloud ALM Mastery
entry.content:
“It just makes work easier and helps you to better understand the designed processes”, Martina Ohlerth says. The IT engineer and product owner works at REWE digital in Germany and oversees the further development of the cloud-based tool for application lifecycle management (ALM), which is available as part of SAP support offerings, covering all stages of the application lifecycle, from discovery of cloud solution capabilities to implementation, operations, and continuous innovation as well as business value realization and enhancement.

“We wanted to preserve our investment with SAP Cloud ALM” 

 With more than 18 years of experience in the field, the 42-year-old has experienced digital transformation first-hand. “To date, we have already achieved a lot with SAP Solution Manager to be more transparent in application lifecycle management. We want to preserve this investment with SAP Cloud ALM.”, Martina explains. “This is the main reason why we are intensively investigating SAP's new tool." 

The mother of three and her small team of four colleagues that is sometimes supported by external consultants embarked on the SAP Cloud ALM journey one and a half years ago. To learn more about the strategy behind the solution, and to find out if it was the right fit for the company, Martina turned to the German SAP-user group DSAG and one of their conferences. “This helped us a lot to find out where we could be going with this”, she explains. 

First learning journey developed with SAP partner  

Liking what they saw, the team began with training for the standard processes. “We turned to live sessions at SAP, looked at the SAP Community and more to get the latest information”, Martina says. When she heard about the beginner-level learning journey “Implementing with SAP Cloud ALM”, available on the SAP Learning site, she immediately saw the potential for everyone on her team to benefit from the free learning resource.  

Created together with SAP partner Vostura, the free, self-paced course is the first that has been developed in collaboration, allowing learners to gain all the necessary knowledge to use SAP Cloud ALM in agile implementation projects – in just under 10 hours. The activities were the starting point for the team's participation in the classroom training “SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation“ to gain direct experience through extensive system exercises with SAP Cloud ALM. 

“In my line of business, it is vital to stay up-to-date with the latest innovations”, Martina highlights, recommending continuous learning for everyone, not just her team. “It just makes work easier and helps you to better understand the designed processes”, she concludes.   

What’s in it for you? 

Are you looking to boost your career and kick-start your own success story? Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on the SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career.

entry.title: Value help in analytical query
entry.content:
Hi all,

In query browser, value help of org division only has one column.

While in VA01, you can see the restriction and output columns of other relevant fields.

Can value help of query also display like that?

And sometimes a query field has relationship to other fields, it looks weird if only one field displays.

 

Regards,
Eric

entry.title: Posting Material Master via OData Service
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am attempting to post a Material Master using the OData service via Postman. However, I am encountering an error message stating: "You have not fully maintained the tax classifications."

I have used the provided body and URL but am unable to resolve this issue. Could you please assist me in identifying what might be missing or incorrect in the request? Any guidance on how to properly maintain the tax classifications would be greatly appreciated.

I only need the body with minimum mandate fields. I have also attached the body which I used to create Material.

 

 
Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Implement SII in Canarias
entry.content:
Hello

I know we need to configure:

  1. Assign SII Attributes to Document Types for IGIC (FIESCAN_DOCTYPEV) (mandatory)
  2. Assign SII Attributes to Tax Codes for IGIC (FIESCAN_TAXCODEV) (mandatory)
  3. Assign SII Attributes to Company Codes (FIESCAN_OTHERV) (optional)
  4. Determine Dates for Incoming Invoices (FIESCAN_COCODEV) (optional)
  5. Change the Handling of Reversal Documents (FIESCAN_REVOUTV) (optional)
  6. Define G/L Accounts Specific to Canary Islands (FIESCAN_GLACCNTV) (optional)
  7. Define Account Keys for Retailer Surcharge on Goods Import (FIESCAN_TAXPROCV) (optional)

Can you please tell me what else or the step by step to implement it?

Thanks

Alfredo

entry.title: SAP Humor
entry.content: Been going through SAP mdk documentation for some time now. Really enjoying giving the questionnaires.  Check out the first option. 😂🤣

entry.title: SAP Energy Park: How EV Charging Enters Our Daily Life - Energy Retail Case
entry.content:
On average, it takes over 66 days to adopt a new habit, and sticking to a new routine can be challenging for many of us.

As humans, we crave an easy and convenient lifestyle. If we want to encourage more people to switch from traditional cars to e-mobility, we need to make EV charging just as convenient as refuelling a car, or even better, integrate it into places where people already spend their time.

One of the biggest challenges in building charging points is finding the space to do so. That's why we're seeing more and more utility companies partnering with shopping center operators and supermarket chains in Germany. However, these partnerships often result in disconnected experiences, only incidentally benefiting everyone involved: the customer, the grocery shop owner, and the utility.

To address this issue, we've teamed up with our colleagues from the SAP Experience Center to create the Walldorf-based SAP Energy Park. Here, we've developed a complete story to illustrate how an EV charge point can be seamlessly integrated into a full end-to-end customer experience at our S.MART Energy Store.

Look at the preview of the Energy Retail story

So, come and explore our very own charge point in front of the SAP Experience Center, and see firsthand how a charging session can enhance the benefits of a loyalty program within a retail collaboration.

The Energy Retail case showcases how charging sessions from our own charge point can create various types of customer interactions, depending on the perspective and the owner of the charge point. We've designed this to integrate with SAP E-Mobility, SAP Cloud for Energy, and CX solutions like SAP Emarsys and SAP Customer Data Platform, allowing us to send push notifications based on current or historical charging sessions.

We're looking at different ways to change consumer perceptions of EV charging and create a meaningful customer experience. For example, we're adding charging sessions to loyalty programs to earn loyalty points and, ultimately, push companies toward a more sustainable energy transition.

We're also showcasing how public charging could be paid for within existing energy contract conditions and how loyalty programs could attract customers on days with high solar power production, perhaps by offering them a voucher to shop during a specific time slot when sustainable energy is readily available.

We are looking forward to welcome you onsite and introduce you to the SAP Energy Park to demonstrate the potential for EV charging to be seamlessly integrated into the customer experience, creating a win-win situation for all involved.

Please follow the link to the SAP Experience Center  to book your tour.

entry.title: Currency Exchange Rate Integration to S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

We are currently trying to find a solution to integrate currency exchange rates from the European Central Bank (ECB) into SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud with Integration Suite (using an API). I wonder if this is possible without using other additional BTP services such as SAP Market Rates Management (BYOR or similar). For example, is it possible to build an integration to import the values to the Currency Exchange Rates app or Import Foreign Exchange Rates app? Maybe building some sort of CDS view, app or similar in S4HC that can fetch the Exchange Rates through an API?

We are not looking for the most efficient way to do this, just to see the opportunity, if it's even possible to get the data into S4HC without using any other additional services than Integration Suite.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP Hybris promotion custom action bean error

entry.title: Re: How to derive the master data values from existing tr...
entry.content:
@azm_azm 

You should provide some version of your code.

Anyway,

Try something like this

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Factory]="A",[d/Account]="Price") !=0 THEN

DATA([d/Type]="X",[d/Account]="Sales Qty")=RESULTLOOKUP([d/Type]="#",[d/Account]="Sales Qty")

ENDIF

 

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
Hi @narendra10041 ,

Solution is available in the blog post ABAP Developer Challenge under 'Solution' section.

entry.title: Re: Error on OData v4 when choosing GridTable
entry.content:
Hi @MioYasutake 

I checked this and the payload is indeed different.

Here I added the column AdditionalDeviceData on ResponsiveTable and v4:
--batch_id-1726470291824-166 Content-Type:application/http Content-Transfer-Encoding:binary GET zc_qm_search?sap-client=100&$count=true&$select=AdditionalDeviceData,NotificationItem,NotificationTask,ObjectNumber,PartnerFunctionName,QualityNotification,ResponsiblePerson&$skip=0&$top=30 HTTP/1.1 Accept:application/json;odata.metadata=minimal;IEEE754Compatible=true Accept-Language:en X-CSRF-Token:nnWVCTJkE1J367YkYZ7Zhg== Content-Type:application/json;charset=UTF-8;IEEE754Compatible=true --batch_id-1726470291824-166-- Group ID: $auto.Workers

Now with GridTable and v4:
--batch_id-1726470371722-144 Content-Type:application/http Content-Transfer-Encoding:binary GET zc_qm_search(QualityNotification='200000000',NotificationTask='0',NotificationItem='1',ObjectNumber='',ResponsiblePerson='',PartnerFunctionName='Author')?sap-client=100&$select=AdditionalDeviceData HTTP/1.1 Accept:application/json;odata.metadata=minimal;IEEE754Compatible=true Accept-Language:en X-CSRF-Token:nnWVCTJkE1J367YkYZ7Zhg== Content-Type:application/json;charset=UTF-8;IEEE754Compatible=true --batch_id-1726470371722-144 Content-Type:application/http Content-Transfer-Encoding:binary GET zc_qm_search(QualityNotification='200000000',NotificationTask='0',NotificationItem='1',ObjectNumber='',ResponsiblePerson='',PartnerFunctionName='Coordinator%20(user)')?sap-client=100&$select=AdditionalDeviceData HTTP/1.1 Accept:application/json;odata.metadata=minimal;IEEE754Compatible=true Accept-Language:en X-CSRF-Token:nnWVCTJkE1J367YkYZ7Zhg== Content-Type:application/json;charset=UTF-8;IEEE754Compatible=true --batch_id-1726470371722-144 Content-Type:application/http Content-Transfer-Encoding:binary GET --batch_id-1726470371722-144-- Group ID: $auto.Workers

I tried this also on a v2 service and GridTable and when I add a new column, the payload looks like the first one. Only a select on the fields and not a search with the keys.

entry.title: IDOC to IDOC(OnPremise) scenarios solution approach ?TPM or IFLOW??
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

 

I need your support to find a good solution approach for the IDOC to IDOC scenarios .

We have many IDOC scenarios, where the IDOC from one ERP (On Premise) system is sent to another ERP system(On Premise)

Since these are IDOC transaction it is possible to implement in TPM.

But I would like to know whether this is recommended by SAP .

Whether TPM or Normal IFLOW is the right solution approach for the IDOC to IDOC scenarios when we are moving to BTP -IS?

entry.title: Can we use personalization action in hybris to populate data in product data only if conditions met?
entry.content:
Hi all,
I have a question related to personalization. I am trying to tackle a issue in hybris where I need to populate data in to the search results product data only if certain conditions are met. Can we do that in hybris with personalization?

Based on results of the personalization conditions we should be able to decide whether to populate a certain field in product data. I have a sticker with text on product listings and this sticket should only be shown on products that are eligible for it. This sticker can be there in personalization action. So if the conditions are passed then we can populate the Product Data field from this sticker in Personalization Action.

entry.title: SAP IAS: Attribute from corporate IdP and map with Role collection SAP BTP
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Is it possible to map user attributes from both IAS and a Corporate IdP to create Role Collections in SAP BTP?

Kind regards,

Leila

entry.title: Transportation Management for own fleet
entry.content:
Good morning/afternoon,

We have a business scenario for transportation management.

In our scenario, our customer owns a large number of vehicles for delivery purposes, where they don’t charge anything for freight. As per our business requirement, we don’t need a subcontracting process.

Additionally, we have an intercompany sales and STO process (1HO and 1P9), which is not supported by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Transportation Management. We would also like to see truck availability.

Would this be possible in the standard system?
Thank You

entry.title: Re: SAC Calculate after aggregation based on datasphere
entry.content:
Can you try doing it locally in SAC using Local Formulas in Stories (Table Widgets)

Open your story and select the table widget where you want to apply a local formula.
Click on the table to select it, then access the widget's menu (usually found in the context menu or on the right-hand side).
Add a Local Formula:Go to the "Edit" mode of the table.
Click on the "Add Calculation" option. This is often found in the table's context menu or data panel.
Define the Formula: You will be presented with a formula editor where you can create your local formula. Use the available dimensions, measures, and operators to define your calculation.

entry.title: Re: How can I delete multiple entries in the CBO? HELP
entry.content:
Hi Mert,

Could you please share the error you faced? In addition to this, you can also delete "all records" of the CBO from below steps, if it works for your scenario. 

Marketing Extensibility app --> CBO --> Delete Records of Custom Business Object

Regards,

Cagri

entry.title: Re: ABAP Test Cockpit in the Cloud - What is already possible
entry.content:
Hi @Chris_Tan ,

it is currently not possible to check the S/4HANA public cloud edition with ATC on BTP. ATC checks in the public cloud edition should be executed directly in the system. See the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/usage-of-abap-test-cockpit-in-the-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13562565 for more details.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: I create sales order and I do delivery when I press p...
entry.content:
Hello,

Do you have a way to check if the issue has generated a log in SM13 transaction?

Personnaly, the last time I had this issue, it was related to a failure with the printout of the delivery note...

Best regards,

Jean-Marie

entry.title: Re: Execute BEx queries at the same time
entry.content: This issue occurs on few workstations. On my WS this situation is floating - sometimes it shows up, sometimes it doesn't.

entry.title: Where is data stored maintained in SAP Build Apps (from Visual Cloud Functions or from the App)
entry.content:
Hello

Questions: 

1/ Where is data stored maintained in SAP Build Apps (from Visual Cloud Functions or from the App) ?

Where and how can we choose the DB on which the data should be stored ? How can we easily do the sizing ?

2/ How can that data be accessed (assuming that it is stored on a DB that can identified)? How can that data be extracted ? Is there a SQL/CDS based access mechanism available ? 

3/ Can we develop SAP Build Apps on a HANA database on which we also develop SAP CAP or SAP RAP ? In other words, can we setup a CDS based data model in 1 SAP HANA DB and then develop above it using CAP or RAP or SAP Build Apps/SAP Build Code where the data model is shared ? (I know that this goes against microservices paradigm, but a coherent CDS based data model on which we develop using the appropriate technology seems to me a good compromise ....)  

Context: 

We are considering to use for example SAP Build Apps to enhance C4C V1 in preparation to move in the future to C4C V2. In this context we were wondering where the data of SAP Build Apps is stored ? Also how would that data be enabled for other usages ? Indeed, we might want to extract that data to Data Warehouse, extract back into C4C V1 for other enablement. It is however not at all clear on how that data can be accessed and exploited. For the moment the SAP Build Apps (for sure the backend features VCF) seems to be a huge black box. Making a nice application with low code/no code is marvelous. But if that is generating hidden data that is not accessible, then we have created a new problem. 

PS: It might be that I am thinking wrongly in the cloud context or that I missed something. My apologies if this would be the case.

entry.title: Re: Error During Preview Application for Fiori Tools Gene...
entry.content:
Hello Christian,

1. try update the generator and/or VSC Extension (regenerate the app)

2. Try execute npm i in terminal and the app/project directory.

And last, try to generate app with yeoman:

npm i -g yeoman-generator
npm i -g @sap/generator-fiori
yo

Then select @sap/fiori and follow the instructions/steps.

entry.title: Re: local setup commece cloud 2211 build failure groovyc
entry.content: Please reinstall hybris and keep the installation directory path as short as possible eg C:\2211\workspace\hybris

entry.title: Re: Issue with Duplicate Invoice Check in Vendor Invoice...
entry.content: Can you view the VIM generated FI Documen in table BSIP, are the matching fields values equal to those of the FB60 manual input invoice (formatting?)

entry.title: Re: Generate Process Models with GenAI
entry.content:
Good news for SAP AI Launchpad users! SAP AI Launchpad can now upload files and supports ChatGPT-4o as well as Claude-Sonnet 3.5.

SAP AI Launchpad

Don't forget to increase the max. token number.

entry.title: Re: Service Orders/Quotations 'Notes' not available for F...
entry.content: Hi Inês, Would you check with your local consultant or developer whether it is possible to defer your logic execution or implementing a post-processing step? For example, by using an update task to delay your logic execution until after the commit, you ensure that the texts are fully saved in their respective tables before trying to access them. This post-processing step will prevent issues related to trying to read uncommitted or previously saved texts. Following this approach, your custom field updates will reflect the latest data, ensuring the "Notes" are accurately captured and displayed in the output form.

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with UI Adaptations for Classic Applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @kmagons 
May I know if it is possible to set fields/columns as visible but 'read only' in public cloud with screen personas ?
If not, Is there any plan for this ?

Thanks and regards,
Corey

entry.title: Assigning the material name into CT04 characteristic
entry.content:
Hello Dear SAP community,

I couldn't find anything relevant to my issue so I decided to create a new question. I am working in the LO-VC environment with the KMATs that have assigned classes, characteristics and so on -of course there are a functional BOMs created under each KMAT.
I need to forward the KMAT name (material description - table MAKT/ field MAKTX) to one of the classes that are present in the KMAT configuration profile. As far as I know it's possible to create a characteristic that has a reference to name field as shown in picture below, but it is only functioning for manual input of the characteristic value that will be forwarded in the procedure/s.
Is there any way to create the characteristic that will just take over the name/parameter that is present in the system?

Thank You in advance
MN

entry.title: Re: Service Orders/Quotations 'Notes' not available for F...
entry.content: Hi Inês, Would you check with your local consultant or developer whether it is possible to defer your logic execution or implementing a post-processing step?  For example, by using an update task to delay your logic execution until after the commit, you ensure that the texts are fully saved in their respective tables before trying to access them. This post-processing step will prevent issues related to trying to read uncommitted or previously saved texts. Following this approach, your custom field updates will reflect the latest data, ensuring the "Notes" are accurately captured and displayed in the output form.

entry.title: Goods issue with wrong unit cost.
entry.content:
Due to wrong foreign exchange rate, my items in the warehouse were posted by wrong item cost. i.e. the item cost escalated by 1000's.  However, the quantity in either GI or GR are correct. Due to this issue, there has been a significant impact on my GL. 

The issues related to Foreign Currency exchange were corrected, however, I am now stuck on how to correct the values of the GR and GI with wrong unit cost need to be corrected. 

I am not sure if I should be going for inventory revaluation or making the reversal entries wherever possible. But the major concern is that the quantity of goods issue or goods receipt is correct.

 

Thank you everyone in advance. #SAPB1

entry.title: Re: SAP IAS: Attribute from corporate IdP and map with Ro...
entry.content: The attribute that is mapped to a BTP Role Collection can be defined in BTP as you like. Just make sure that the attribut is contained in the Token or SAML Assertion. Of course the default way is to use the groups attribute which can be filled with all the groups a user is assigned to.

entry.title: 8 Steps of EWM Migration Business Suite 9.x to Embedded EWM on S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello EWM Folks 

In this blog, we will explore the key prerequisites and steps for migrating from the EWM Business Suite to Embedded EWM on S/4 HANA. EWM is a strategic warehouse management solution from SAP. Since the launch of Embedded EWM with the S/4HANA 1809 release, SAP has been supported their customers running warehouses on Business Suite 9.x in their digital transformation. the ability to simplify their landscape by offering the option to deploy EWM in an embedded 

Choosing between Decentralized EWM and Embedded EWM involves a comprehensive analysis from various perspectives—not just only from functionality- several non-functional criteria must be evaluated, including understanding the business vision, assessing transition risks, considering costs, and evaluating maintainability..ect  While we won't delve into these aspects in this blog, it's crucial to evaluate that as important prerequisites.

Let’s begin with a quick overview and recap of the available deployment options for EWM. This has been discussed in various blogs, but we’ll provide a brief recap here.

 

  1. Embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA

EWM is tightly integrated on same SAP S/4HANA box, providing a seamless and efficient solution for warehouse management, Offers basic / Advanced warehouse management functionalities, suitable for mid-sized warehouses with standard processes. May have limitations in handling very large or complex high volume warehouses 

     2 .Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA

EWM runs on a separate server platform, providing more flexibility and scalability. Offers advanced warehouse management features, suitable for large-scale warehouses with complex processes. Connects to SAP S/4HANA via RFCs and IDOC .  

Let us also have a look into the license part Embedded EWM : -

in Embedded EWM there are two different licenses based on the functionality that will run , Basic or Advanced EWM 

Basic EWM is  included in the core SAP S/4HANA license. This means that organizations using SAP S/4HANA might not incur additional costs for Basic EWM.

Advanced EWM typically requires a separate license. This means organizations need to purchase additional licenses to access the advanced features and capabilities.

With the Basic Warehouse Management usage mode, you can use the following basic warehousing processes
and functions:

• Inventory Management
• Inbound Processing (for example, Deconsolidation)
• Outbound Processing
• Internal Warehouse Movements
• Physical Inventory
• Reporting

With the Extended Warehouse Management usage mode, you can additionally use the following advanced
warehousing functions:

• Inventory Management Optimization (for example, Slotting)
• Inbound Process Optimization
• Outbound Process Optimization (for example, Waves)
• Material Flow Control
• Yard Management (for example, TU handling, Dock Appointment Scheduling)
• Labor Management
• Value Added Services
• Kitting

Before jump into the major step of EWM Embedded Migration ,Question here what are the major differences between between EWM on business suite and Embedded EWM

 

 

 

Master Data :-

in Embedded EWM No Cor Interface , which mean its not require to Master Data distribution ( Material , Batches , Classifications , Business partners ). EWM Tables /SAPAPO/MATMAP , /SAPAPO/MATKEY,   /SAPAPO/MATTXT ,  /SAPAPO/MARM , /SAPAPO/MEANGTIN , /SCMB/MATID_MAP , /SAPAPO/MATEXEC its read S/4 HANA tables directly MARA , MAKT , MARM , MEAN .

However, there are certain warehouse-dependent tables that require manual maintenance for data such as /SAPAPO/MATAPN.

DDIC Objects & Tables :-

Embedded EWM eliminates the need for redundant configuration tables, as the following tables become cross-functional across all S/4HANA applications, including EWM:

 

Catch-weight tolerance group

CW profile for CW quantities

Delivery priority

Handling indicator

Handling unit type

Incoterms

Packing group

Quality inspection group

Serial number profile

Shipping conditions

Transportation group

Warehouse material group

Warehouse storage condition

Transaction Data Distribution : -

Despite QRFC remaining an option for delivery-based integration between S/4HANA and EWM, synchronized goods movements offer an alternative that does not require document distribution. Supported scenarios for goods receipt and goods issue processes can be posted using MIGO. For more information about synchronized goods movements, please refer to this link 

Functionality : -

When deciding on the product version for a new EWM implementation project, SAP recommends carefully reviewing the differences between decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA Release XXXX and SAP EWM 9.x.

If you refer to the usual SAP release notes regarding information and restrictions for decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA, you will find an important attachment. The note and its attached document, "EWM Deployment Differences," outline the feature parity gaps between decentralized EWM on S/4HANA and SAP EWM 9.5. Additionally, decentralized EWM on S/4HANA includes new features that are not available in SAP EWM 9.5. SAP has published these new features for each S/4HANA release in the "What's New Viewer - SAP S/4HANA," which is accessible through the SAP Help Portal. All SAP S/4HANA versions after 1709 offer more functionality than SAP EWM 9.5.

Also Its important also to check the compatibility of existing Add-on whether that's compatible with S/4 or not For a list of supported add-ons, see SAP Note 2214409. For information about uninstalling add-ons, see SAP Note 2011192. 

Innovation : -

Embedded analytics was not available on business suite , but know customers able to access and analyze real-time data directly within the EWM system, without the need for separate reporting tools. This provides warehouse managers and other stakeholders with a comprehensive overview of  their KPIs and operational metrics , 

Warehouse insight  is an SAP Cloud based tool that leverages advanced algorithms and machine learning to optimize warehouse operations and resource utilization. It provides real-time insights, KPI analysis, and simulation capabilities to enhance warehouse efficiency.

 

7 Steps Migration procedure : -

 

 

Step #1 System Setup

In this step its contain basic sittings system for EWM in SAP S/4HANA. from the following aspects of the configuration:
Define logical system, Business system, System Configuration of qRFC communication between SAP EWM and other SAP S/4HANA applications ,Technical Prerequisites for qRFC Communication Creating ,Dummy Logical System Manually .

Definition of a warehouse and integration of the warehouse in the enterprise structure Configuration, and complete configuration relevant , align number ranges from HU and Business Partners 

Step #2 Custom Code 

By using SE03 Transport Organizer you can transport custom code from the source to the target system , then Run ATC check or perform Custom Code Impact Analysis for the custom codes to identify necessary codes to be changed.

Step #3 Manage and compare common 13 Customize table

If you remember in this blog there will be 13 customizing table shared across different application , Compare the Business Suite EWM table content with the ERP table entries and adjust/create missing entries in ERP.

Step #4 Manage other Local Customizing

In this step perform Customizing transport for existing EWM confirmation and activate BC-Sets

Step #5 Role & Authorization

By using TCODE PFCG .It is possible to put all roles in the source system into a transport  and import it transport into the target system S/4HANA

Step #6 Migrate Local Master Data

Since Migration of different master data become available in different ways whether using staging table or Direct Transfer in Migration cockpit  With SAP S/4HANA 2020 10 more objects were introduced and with 2021 and 2022 again a few new objects. From SAP S/4HANA 2022 on, all these migrations objects are also supported in EWM embedded in S/4 HANA.However there are local master data a part from standard migration which need to be investigated , like if the master data has custom fields , is these are client or warehouse specific or not ..ect to define the way of migrating these data .

Step #7 Migrate Stock 

There are different ways to Migrate stocks whether by using Direct transfer which enables you to migrate warehouse stock data from the source EWM system to the target SAP S/4HANA system, based on the default selection criteria set for the migration object. This migration technique posts data to the target system using a function module.or using initial stock upload /SCWM/ISU

Step #8 Close Document  

All transactional documents need to be completed or closed before migration .

entry.title: Re: How to use XSUAA in FastAPI Python app
entry.content: The tutorial Create an Application with Cloud Foundry Python Buildpack provides an example using Flask. Maybe you can adopt that to FastAPI. Would be great if you share it afterwards.

entry.title: Top Cypress Testing Errors and How to Avoid Them
entry.content:
The first one that will be described is the error related to cy.clearCokies().

This kind of error typically occurs in the context of Cypress, which is a JavaScript-based end-to-end testing framework. Cypress is used for testing web applications, and cy.clearCookies() is a command that clears all the cookies that are stored for the current domain. 

Possible Causes of cy.clearCookies() Error: 

Context or Domain Issues: The error may arise if Cypress attempts to clear cookies outside the context of the domain being tested. cy.clearCookies() is domain-specific, meaning it can only clear cookies that are set for the domain the test is currently running on. If there is a domain mismatch or if the test is not currently interacting with a domain, the command might fail. 

Misconfigured Cypress Settings: Sometimes, the error might be due to incorrect or missing configuration settings in your Cypress tests, such as not specifying the right base URL (Universal Resource Locators) or domain, which can confuse the command about which cookies to clear. 

Third-Party Cookies: Clearing third-party cookies can also cause issues, especially if the cookies are not accessible due to cross-origin restrictions. If your test interacts with multiple domains or subdomains, you might need to manage cookies more explicitly. 

Timing Issues: The error might occur if cy.clearCookies() is called at an inappropriate time during the test, such as before the application fully loads or before any cookies have been set. 

Browser-Specific Behaviour: Different browsers might handle cookies differently, especially when it comes to privacy settings or cross-origin policies. This can lead to inconsistent behaviour when clearing cookies. 

Debugging Steps: 

Check Domain: Ensure that the test is running on the expected domain when cy.clearCookies() is called. 

Use cy.clearCookies({ domain: "yourdomain.com" }): Explicitly specify the domain to avoid domain mismatch issues. 

Check Timing: Make sure cy.clearCookies() is called after the page has loaded and cookies have been set. 

Browser Configuration: Verify that your test settings (like browser settings) are not interfering with cookie handling. 

Look at Cypress Logs: Check the Cypress logs to see if there is any additional information about why the error occurred. 

If the problem persists, reviewing the specific error message in the Cypress console can provide more insights into what might be going wrong. 

 

The second type of error that often occurs is related to the cy.click() command. 

This kind of error happens because the element you are trying to click on is being covered by another element. 

Breakdown of the Error: 

Error Message: "Timed out retrying after 30050ms: cy.click() failed because this element is being covered by another element." 

Specific Element: The element in question is an SVG icon with the following attributes: 
html 
Copy code 
<svg class="MuiSvgIcon-root MuiSvgIcon-colorSuccess MuiSvgIcon-fontSizeMedium css-nm8jzx" ...> 

Issue: The error indicates that Cypress tried to click on the specified SVG element, but it was unable to do so because another element (in this case, an HTML element) is covering it. 

Common Causes: 

Overlapping Elements: Another element (like a modal, loading spinner or an invisible overlay) might be positioned on top of the element you are trying to interact with. 

Timing Issues: The page might not be fully loaded or an animation might still be in progress, causing the element to be temporarily covered when Cypress attempts to click on it. 

Incorrect Element Detection: Sometimes, Cypress might be trying to click on a different element than intended, due to similar classes or IDs. 

Solutions: 

Wait for the Element to be Uncovered: Use cy.wait() or wait for a specific condition to ensure the element is not covered when Cypress attempts to click on it. 

Example: 
javascript 
Copy code 
cy.get('your-element-selector').should('be.visible').click(); 

Scroll into View: If the element is off-screen or partially hidden, you can scroll it into view before clicking. 

Example: 
javascript 
Copy code 
cy.get('your-element-selector').scrollIntoView().click(); 

Force Click: As a last resort, you can bypass the error by using {force: true} in your cy.click() command. This forces the click even if the element is covered. 

Example: 
javascript 
Copy code 
cy.get('your-element-selector').click({ force: true }); 

Caution: While this can solve the problem, it is better to resolve the underlying issue (like an element being covered) rather than forcing a click. 

Check for Overlapping Elements: Ensure there are no overlapping elements like popups, modals or invisible divs that could interfere with the click. 

By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the error and ensure Cypress interacts with the correct element during your tests. 

In summary it is crucial to name the most potential causes of the described errors and to get to know the solution, what can prevent the errors appearance in the first place.

entry.title: Re: ATP check for sto purchase orders
entry.content:
Hi Zareena,

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition you can find the setting for STO in the SSCUIs. For this, open the app "Manage Your Solution" and navigate to the SSCUI with the name 'Configure Delivery Type & Availability Check Procedure by Plant' (its SSCUI ID is 103424). Do do this, you can filter for Application Area 'Sourcing and Procurement' and Item 'Purchase Order Processing'.

You can find the so-called Checking Rule maintained. By standard content it is 'A'. This translates to the same ATP Scope of Check settings shown in your screenshot. 

Regards,
Andreas

entry.title: How to replace BW/HANA Composite Provider with new Source Object
entry.content:
Hello All,

In HANA calculation view , we can replace source table X with the some other Table Y easily by using the Option "Replace with DataSource" & in next pop-up/screen it gives me the flexibility to map the columns b/w old table X & the new table Y.

This way no further adjustments required in that CV if we could map all of the required columns.

The same option , I am trying at HANA Composite Provider to replace one source with the new one using the below "replace" option.. But its not giving any option to map the columns b/w old vs new sources. It simply removing the entire source & replacing it with the new one. All of the existing mapping from that Old source are removed.

Is this the expected behavior w.r.t HANA HCPR ? if yes , how can we handle this scenarios in a better way without loosing the existing mappings from the old source.

Thanks

Kiran K

entry.title: Re: Query to list components and unit price
entry.content:
Hello Rah,

you must use the following link to the ITT1 table:

LEFT JOIN ITT1 T2 ON T1.[ItemCode] = T2.[Code] AND T0.ItemCode = T2.Father

But since you said that "there are multiple phantom part numbers in the BOM", then a clear link to the ITT1 table is no longer possible. That won't work.

Best regards

Inga

entry.title: Re: 1H 2021 release - impacting on online item and displa...
entry.content:
Hi,

Were you able to find solution for this issue?

entry.title: Re: How can I delete multiple entries in the CBO? HELP
entry.content:
Hi Cagri,

 

I use the same code like from the link. 

Regards,

Mert

entry.title: Re: How to use Standard API from BAS
entry.content: I would think the SAPUI5 tutorials for BAS should be able to guide you.

entry.title: Re: Assigning the material name into CT04 characteristic
entry.content:
Hi!
I can confirm that this works when setting up the characteristic as you have explained and assigning it to a 001 class which then is assigned to a material.
Since you're referring to the configuration profile, I assume you're using class type 300? If the functionality does not work for class type 300, you might want to look into a function module integrated via dependency knowledge that fetches the description.

Best Regards,
Felix

entry.title: Re: Where comes libraries.csv from?
entry.content: See below

entry.title: Created an event in MDK project after that deleted the page getting error don't find reference file
entry.content: Created an event in MDK project after that deleted the page getting error don't find reference file how to overcome the error

entry.title: Re: How to use XSUAA in FastAPI Python app
entry.content: @gregorw, thank you for the prompt help.. But the problem with the approach defined above is we need to create a separate webapp for user landing, then forward the user to the actual fastapi app. The fastapi alone is capable to having the frontend templates, then do we really need to have a separate node app(app router) just for landing the user?

entry.title: Re: Find API based interfaces in a SAP system in question
entry.content:
Which APIs are you interested in? I think there are different transactions/search capabilities depending on the type of APIs.

E.g. IDocs, RFC-enabled function modules, BAPIs, WebServices, OData Services, etc.

entry.title: Re: Unable to laod SAP Build Process Automation - In Main...
entry.content:
Hello Adam,

Thank you for reaching out to us. Are you still experiencing issues with the maintenance message? Please let us know if you need any further clarification.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
My name is Hussain and I can identify myself in following category:

*People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I want to change my career from Telecommunications industry to SAP. And since i got the opportunity to start a new job in a company who is using SAP, i want to be prepared for SAP analytics and to show them my interest and pursue my career in SAP analytics field.

entry.title: SAP Discovery Center at SAP TechEd 2024: Sessions, Resources, and Highlights
entry.content:
Planning, preparations, and excitement are all in full swing for SAP TechEd 2024! The event will kick-off virtually before heading out on a global tour, making attendance easier than ever. The session catalog is filled with hot topics and content experts, eager to share the latest innovations and news coming out of SAP. If you haven’t done so already, register for SAP TechEd 2024.

Whether you are new to SAP Business Technology Platform or a returning veteran, SAP Discovery Center is your go-to resource. From detailed use cases and service information to step-by-step missions and reference architectures, the Discovery Center helps you realize the full potential of SAP BTP, accelerating product adoption and driving informed decisions. Haven’t explored it yet? Now’s the perfect time! Go to SAP Discovery Center.

TechEd Sessions Featuring SAP Discovery Center

For this year’s SAP TechEd, Discovery Center will be featured, including deep dive demos, in the following sessions:

Session XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center
Explore the latest enhancements in speed of project implementation and personalization in SAP Discovery Center. See the powerful advantages of guided implementation projects and reference architectures curated by SAP. Learn how to effectively use the service catalog in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), the extensive list of real-world use cases, and your control center. Whether a new or experienced user, you’ll get the knowledge and tools to accelerate your SAP BTP journey.
Speakers: Nis Boy Naeve and Marco Geldmacher

Session XP105 | Start your journey with SAP BTP and confidently accelerate adoption
Are you new to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) or seeking guidance on best practices and architectural decisions? Take a look at the powerful advantages of guided implementation projects curated by SAP experts and partners with SAP Discovery Center. Discover how the SAP BTP Guidance Framework assists you with decision guides, reference architectures, and best practices to design, develop, and operate enterprise-grade applications on SAP BTP.
Speakers: Konstantin Heine and Cecily Sorenson

Session XP204 | Modernize your technology landscape with SAP BTP and SAP LeanIX solutions
Discover how SAP LeanIX solutions can transform your as-is technology landscape leveraging SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). Learn how to get instant visibility in your as-is SAP landscape using automated application discovery. Model your SAP BTP landscape with best practices, define your to-be technology landscape with the help of reference content based on SAP BTP Guidance Framework, and manage your technology transformation, governance compliance, and risks using report templates.
Speakers: Katrin von Ahsen and Jens Rieskamp

Don’t Miss the SAP TechEd Learning Zone

It wouldn’t be TechEd without a core emphasis on learning and upskilling opportunities. This year’s TechEd Learning Zone features tailored learning experiences for each content track. Within each track you will also find reference to six key use cases. These use cases have been hand-selected for their topic relevance and quality guidance.

More About SAP BTP Use Cases

SAP BTP use cases consist of customer-proven, well-documented missions for an easy implementation of SAP BTP. They support you in solving problems in a repeatable manner to achieve the desired business outcome faster. To learn more about what SAP BTP looks like in real life and how it drives business innovation and success, explore the featured use cases.

What’s New in SAP Discovery Center

Exciting new features are coming to SAP Discovery Center! Stay tuned by subscribing to this blog post or following me on LinkedIn to be one of the first to know—you won’t want to miss it!

entry.title: Restriction for Input screen in organization fields
entry.content:
We have a request from the Business to apply restrictions on the input screen for Fiori apps. For example, the user should only see the company code 1208 in the input screen. However, currently, all users can view other company codes available in the system, despite the restrictions being set in the role for all access categories

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Restriction for Input screen in organization fields
entry.content:
Hello,

Kindly refer to the below KBA:

 

https://me.sap.com/notes/3505645

 

Thank you

SAP SUPPORT

entry.title: Re: Where comes libraries.csv from?
entry.content: I'm clicking from Windows Explorer or Total Commander (a file manager), and the issue appears in both cases. I'm using Crystal Reports 2020 SP03 PL06. On the machines where this occurs, only Windows Defender is installed. The executable I'm using is: C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64\crw64.exe. Currently, no file logging is enabled. The Libraries.csv file appears only in conjunction with Crystal Reports, and not with other file types like *.docx or *.pdf.

entry.title: Re: Assign default value to field in Fiori Elements
entry.content: I need to do it from the frontend since I need to get the user (sap.ushell.Container.getService("UserInfo")) from the IAS to set it in a field that is the requisitioner name in a requisition

entry.title: Re: Material Master Nomenclature
entry.content:
It is basically a design change, now you need to play around with Material Type and Material Group, Main Category differentiate by Material type, and then Sub Category by Material Group.

For rest of the information you can use material description and for further categorization use Characteristics. 

thanks,

entry.title: Re: Missing "yellow total line" and "Change layout button" in ME51N/ME52N
entry.content: Hello Zoran,
